title: Mock Exam B: System Review And Hardening
description: A redesigned RHCSA v9 mock exam focused on local storage, default permissions, SELinux labeling and port control, process tuning, logging, and file workflows.

tasks: Use the attached data disks to create volume group opsdata_vg, an XFS logical volume named records sized to 700 MiB mounted persistently at /srv/records, an ext4 logical volume named archive sized to 300 MiB mounted persistently at /mnt/archive, and a 256 MiB swap logical volume named reviewswap activated at boot.
Set the system-wide default umask to 027 and adjust /srv/reports so it is owned by root:reviewers, group writable, inaccessible to others, and setgid for new files.

Make Apache serve /srv/rhcsa/review-site/index.html successfully at http://localhost:8089/ while SELinux remains enforcing, using persistent file context and port-label changes, set httpd_can_network_connect persistently to on, and keep the service enabled at boot.

Lower the priority of the seeded CPU-intensive process without stopping it, configure persistent journaling, activate the throughput-performance tuned profile, and enable and start review-stamp.service.
Create /root/reports-bundle.tar.gz from /srv/reports and extract only the ALERT lines from /opt/rhcsa/workspaces/review-material/alerts.log into /root/alerts-only.log.
hints: The storage work is local to clientvm and should survive reboot through fstab and swap configuration.
Serving content from /srv usually needs both Apache configuration changes and persistent SELinux labeling.
This exam also expects a custom SELinux http port label and a persistent SELinux boolean change.
The file-work task is separate from the storage task; use the seeded /srv/reports content.
solution_outline: Complete the LVM work first to establish the required filesystems and swap entries.
Persist the umask in a shell startup file, fix /srv/reports ownership and permissions, and then repair Apache plus SELinux for the custom site path and port.
Finish by renicing the busy process, making journald persistent, switching the tuned profile, enabling the custom service, and creating the archive and filtered log output.

tasks: Create volume group opsdata_vg with an XFS logical volume records mounted at /srv/records, an ext4 logical volume archive mounted at /mnt/archive, and a 256 MiB swap logical volume named reviewswap activated persistently.
Set the system-wide default umask to 027 and make /srv/reports collaborative for group reviewers only, with setgid for new files.

Make Apache serve /srv/rhcsa/review-site/index.html at http://localhost:8089/ while SELinux remains enforcing, apply persistent file and port labeling, set httpd_can_network_connect persistently to on, and ensure the service starts at boot.
Lower the priority of the seeded busy process, persist the journal, activate throughput-performance, and enable and start review-stamp.service.
Create /root/reports-bundle.tar.gz from /srv/reports and write only the ALERT lines from /opt/rhcsa/workspaces/review-material/alerts.log to /root/alerts-only.log.
